title: Conganige Miyuki Anthony
content:
Conganige Miyuki Anthony

Welty’s short story “A Worn Path” is abundant with symbolism that exaggerates themes of perseverance and sacrifice through Phoenix Jackson’s journey. The narrative follows an elderly Black woman who braves a prolonged and strenuous way to get medicine for her sick grandson, showing her resolute determination and selflessness.

One of the most meaningful symbols in the story is Phoenix Jackson’s name. The phoenix, a mythical bird that rises from its ashes, represents renewal and resilience. Just as the phoenix endures destruction only to be reborn, Phoenix Jackson overcomes numerous obstacles, both physical and societal on her journey. Despite her age and weakness, she moves forward, emphasizing the spirit of determination. At one point, after struggling to cross a log over a creek, she says, “I wasn’t as old as I thought” (Welty.P.3). This moment undoubtedly shows her inner strength and tenacity, proving the symbolism of the phoenix as a figure of resilience.

The path is also considered another powerful symbol, which portrays both Phoenix’s physical journey and the battles of life. The obstacles and problems she face through the way reflect broader societal and personal hardships. For instance, when she becomes entangled in a thorny bush, she remarks, “Thorns, you doing your appointed work. Never want to let folks pass, no sir” (Welty.P.3). This moment spotlights how life’s challenges often seem intentionally placed to hinder progress, yet she perseveres regardless. The worn path is also evidence of the repetitive nature of her journey, just as she has walked this road many times before, she will repeatedly do so as long as her grandson needs her.
